compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Collapse the agent's monitoring to ONE call returning ONE typed outcome, so agents never correlate votes/labels/status â€” and the "both votes green but conflicts" trap is handled inside the tool, not by every session.

## What
A project command (project CLI/script, NOT `rebar` core; may `import rebar` only for ticket ops) that requests a land and returns one typed terminal outcome + distinct exit code. Blocking `--wait` + single-shot `land-status`.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S1** (`sterilized-papery-horse`, 843b-801f-9ef9-442d) â€” the `Autosubmit` label, which `land` sets â€” and **S3** (`darksome-starry-sable`, 0525-6d71-8969-4512) â€” the recorded outcome, the authoritative source this command reads before falling back to native votes.

**Status-surface model (prior art: Chromium CQ + Zuul/Tide).** Two channels, mirroring how CQ and Zuul report:
- **Per-change outcome â†’ the Gerrit change itself.** The bot reports each change's result BACK onto the change â€” a review comment + the `Autosubmit` label state (CQ's "reset" pattern) â€” so `land` reads `merged`/`needs_rebase`/`ci_failed`/`review_failed`/`abandoned` via the Gerrit REST creds it already holds, together with the S3 marker. No bespoke per-change status channel.
- **Bot liveness + queue-status â†’ a read-only HTTP status endpoint** on the bot (Zuul `zuul-web` / Tide `deck` style; S5 AC6). `land`/`land-status` GET it for the `lander_down` liveness read and the queue view. The local heartbeat file backs the in-container HEALTHCHECK; the status endpoint surfaces that heartbeat's age to remote clients as the JSON field **`heartbeat_age_s`** (integer seconds since the last heartbeat write) â€” the shared field name S4 and S5 both bind to.

**Interface dependency on S5** (`perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon`, 3c55-2af3-0c9c-4b11): `land` reads liveness + queue-status from S5's read-only status endpoint (exposing the heartbeat age against a **90 s staleness threshold**). This is an interface-contract dependency (endpoint shape + staleness threshold), NOT a build-order blocker â€” `land` is coded against the contract and unit-tested with a fake endpoint.

## Scope
The agent-facing command (`land` / `land-status`, at `infra/autolander/land.py`) + the outcome enum contract. Lives in project tooling, not `src/rebar`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `land <change> --wait` **checks the bot heartbeat FIRST and only sets `Autosubmit` if the heartbeat is fresh** â€” if the heartbeat is already stale at invocation it returns `lander_down` **without** setting the label (never leave an orphaned `Autosubmit` the down bot will not consume). When fresh, it sets `Autosubmit` **under the agent's OWN Gerrit identity** (a Contributor â€” requires S1's requester-votable ACL; no bot-side voting endpoint), blocks until terminal, and returns one typed outcome as JSON + a **distinct exit code per outcome** per this table: `0 merged`, `1 needs_rebase`, `2 ci_failed`, `3 review_failed`, `4 not_requested` (land-status only â€” no `Autosubmit` set and no bot record), `5 abandoned`, `6 lander_down`, `7 error` (any other failure), `75 pending` (land-status only â€” bot still driving), `124 timed_out`. Every exit code is pinned (no "any nonzero" hole).
- [ ] **`lander_down` (bot-SPOF liveness):** the single-instance bot is a SPOF; if it is down/wedged, `land --wait`/`land-status` must NOT hang to `timed_out`. Both read the bot's liveness from S5's **read-only status endpoint** (which exposes the heartbeat age; the bot writes the heartbeat once per its 15 s poll â€” see S2); if the reported heartbeat is stale â€” **older than 90 s** (6Ă— the 15 s poll, so a few consecutive slow iterations / a transient hiccup do NOT trip a false `lander_down`) â€” they return the distinct `lander_down` outcome + exit code (`6`) IMMEDIATELY (within one `land` poll cycle, not after the 30-min timeout), with the message "lander unavailable â€” rebase to tip and submit manually (see CONTRIBUTING Â§2e 'Submit', ADR-0040)." **If the status endpoint is unreachable** (connection refused/timeout), that is treated conservatively as `lander_down` (liveness cannot be confirmed), NOT as `error`. The manual FFO rebase-and-submit is the sanctioned degraded path. *(These operational thresholds â€” 90 s staleness, 30 s poll, 30 min timeout â€” are config values coupled to S5's 15 s bot poll; they are read from config, not hard-coded literals, so an S5 poll-rate change can be matched without a code edit.)*
- [ ] `land-status <change>` single-shot variant returns the same enum **plus the non-terminal `pending`** value while the bot is still driving (it does NOT set `Autosubmit` â€” that is `land`'s job; this resolves the fire-and-forget-then-check-back flow: run `land <change>` once, poll `land-status` which returns `pending` until terminal). `land-status` applies the **same fallback-precedence rule as AC5**. On a change with **no bot record and no `Autosubmit` set**, it returns the native-derived terminal outcome if the change is already merged/abandoned/failing; otherwise `not_requested` (exit `4`) â€” signalling the agent to run `land` first.
- [ ] **`--wait` termination/timeout:** every 30s the poll loop consults BOTH (a) native Gerrit state via `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS` (the `o=DETAILED_LABELS` option is REQUIRED to return per-label vote values; a bare `GET /changes/{id}` returns status + revision only, not label votes) for `merged`/`ci_failed`/`review_failed`/`abandoned` (MERGED / ABANDONED / a failing label), AND (b) the auto-lander's recorded outcome (S3, read via its status query â€” a read of its state dir / a small status endpoint) for `needs_rebase`, which is NOT derivable from native state (under FFO a behind-tip change is *non-submittable* per ADR-0040, but `submittable=false` alone does not distinguish behind-tip from a failing gate vote); it does NOT rely on `GET /changes/{id}` alone. A Gerrit **`ABANDONED`** status maps to the terminal `abandoned` outcome with its own distinct exit code (`5`); overall timeout 30 min â†’ `timed_out` (exit `124`); transport errors are retried (bounded) before surfacing `error`.
- [ ] **Fallback precedence** for deriving the outcome: (1) the bot's recorded outcome (S3) wins if present; else derive from native Gerrit state â€” `Verified -1` â†’ `ci_failed`, `LLM-Review -1` â†’ `review_failed`, MERGED â†’ `merged`, `ABANDONED` â†’ `abandoned`; if both `-1` are present with no bot record, `ci_failed` wins. **`needs_rebase` is NOT derivable from native state** â€” under FFO (ADR-0040) a behind-tip change is *non-submittable* (`submittable=false`), but `submittable=false` alone does not distinguish behind-tip (â†’ `needs_rebase`, a clean rebase fixes it) from a failing gate vote (â†’ `ci_failed`/`review_failed`); FFO surfaces the behind-tip case as a "not fast-forward" refusal only on the submit *attempt*, so `needs_rebase` requires the bot's recorded outcome (S3); it never appears via native-state fallback.
- [ ] Command lives in PROJECT tooling (repo CLI/script), NOT `rebar` core; may `import rebar` only for ticket ops (e.g. close the ticket on merge). No Gerrit-specific landing logic in `src/rebar`. **Gerrit REST access reuses this repo's proven STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit-client approach** â€” the `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` pattern (`urllib` + HTTP Basic auth + XSSI stripping, *"deliberately NO new httpx dependency"*): NOT a new third-party client (not `pygerrit2`) and NOT a from-scratch reimplementation. Packaged as a **thin stdlib helper in `infra/autolander`** (mirrors the proven `urllib`+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency; honors the project-tooling/core boundary; unification behind the platform provider-seam is deferred to idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`). Auth is the **agent's OWN ambient Gerrit HTTP credential** read from the standard place (`.netrc` / the git credential helper the agent already uses to push to Gerrit) and passed as a stdlib `urllib` Basic-auth header â€” no separate credential-injection mechanism to invent.
- [ ] The outcome enum is documented as a **stable, versioned contract** at `docs/land-contract.md` â€” listing the enum values, the exit-code table, the **JSON output schema** (the shape of the `land`/`land-status` JSON â€” `{outcome, change, detail?, â€¦}` â€” since AC1 makes JSON the machine interface agents parse), and a `contract-version`; `land --help` cites it. Agents depend only on this contract. (The ADR that records the overall auto-lander design and anchors this contract is authored downstream by S6, not here.)
- [ ] **Verification/tests:** unit tests cover the fallback-precedence rule (S3-record-wins, native-state derivation, the `ci_failed`-wins tie-break), the heartbeat-freshness gate (freshâ†’set label; staleâ†’`lander_down` with no label set; read-errorâ†’`lander_down`), every outcomeâ†’exit-code mapping, and `land-status` on a never-requested change. A live/E2E check runs `land` against one real change on the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (guarding against mock-only proxy validation). Recorded as `set_verify_commands` DD entries.
compiled_state.title: S4 Agent landing contract: land/land-status command + typed outcome enum (project tooling)

verify_commands.dd_text: land returns typed outcome JSON + distinct exit code
